title: The rise of AI drives memory chip manufacturers and boosts valuations
summary: Memory chip manufacturers benefit from the rise of artificial intelligence, with rising prices and Kioxia employees becoming billionaires, while sector valuations soar globally.

title: Massive spending on AI raises doubts about its sustainability and impact on buybacks
summary: Big tech companies invest billions in artificial intelligence, but analysts warn that it could displace buybacks and that only 3% of households pay for these services.

title: Escalation of the conflict between the US and Iran: attacks on facilities and threats in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: The US attacks Iranian military installations in retaliation, while mine warnings paralyze traffic in the Strait of Hormuz for months.

title: Russia faces economic pressures as the EU debates the gas ban and the stock market falls
summary: A major Spanish gas hub calls for delaying the ban on Russian gas, while Russian stocks close lower amid sanctions.

title: Recommendations for investing in artificial intelligence after a key stock's drop
summary: An AI stock falls 13% in a month and is considered a buying opportunity, while experts propose three ways to invest in the sector through funds.

title: Growing retirement concerns: Social Security benefits could be reduced and Americans need more savings
summary: Social Security benefits in the US could decrease by 2035, while Americans estimate they need $1.46 million to retire, a record figure.

title: Movements in the healthcare sector: big IPO of a biotech company and sale of QuidelOrtho's unit
summary: An unknown biotech company achieves a market debut surpassing SpaceX, while QuidelOrtho seeks to sell its testing unit amid private equity interest.

title: Pimco and UBS present investment strategies in an uncertain market
summary: Pimco boosts private placements while UBS publishes its portfolio guide to navigate market uncertainty.

title: Extreme weather phenomena: deadly heatwave in Europe and 'dry rain'
summary: A heatwave in Paris causes 109 deaths and Germany records record temperatures, while the 'dry rain' phenomenon is reported elsewhere.

title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000 and analysts look to the past
summary: The largest cryptocurrency on the market breaks the $60,000 support, while experts analyze historical patterns to predict its next move.

title: Tesla reaches a key milestone in the development of its artificial intelligence chip
summary: The company completes a significant advance in its AI chip, reinforcing its expansion strategy beyond the automotive sector.

title: GameStop maintains its offer for eBay despite the company board's rejection
summary: The video game chain continues its attempt to acquire the auction platform, despite opposition from eBay's board.

title: Subsidiary of Persistent Systems launches a takeover bid for Nagarro at 81 euros per share
summary: A subsidiary of India's Persistent Systems has presented a public takeover offer for the consulting firm Nagarro, valued at 81 euros per share.

title: Millions of investors will indirectly own shares of SpaceX, whether they want to or not
summary: A new investment vehicle will allow millions of shareholders to have exposure to SpaceX without buying shares directly, generating debate.

title: Amazon's Prime Day reveals US consumer behavior with tight budgets
summary: The discount event shows how consumers in the US manage their spending in a context of inflation and lower purchasing power.

title: Hong Kong stock sales hit five-year high driven by AI
summary: The equity placement market in Hong Kong records its highest level since 2021, thanks to appetite for artificial intelligence companies.

title: Geely will send the first electric Lotus vehicles to Canada in July under Carney-Xi agreement
summary: Chinese manufacturer Geely will export Lotus electric vehicles to Canada as part of a bilateral trade agreement, according to the ambassador.

title: Magnitude 6.1 earthquake shakes northeastern Japan without tsunami warning
summary: A magnitude 6.1 earthquake affected the Aomori and Iwate prefectures, with seismic intensity 5, without tsunami risks.

title: Japanese singer and actor Minowa Akihiro dies at 91
summary: Minowa Akihiro, known for his songs and theatrical performances, died at 91, leaving a cultural legacy in Japan.

title: Consecutive earthquakes in Venezuela leave more than 1,400 dead and thousands missing
summary: A series of strong earthquakes in Venezuela has caused a catastrophe with more than 1,400 fatalities and numerous missing persons.
